what_it_does: Renders a modal floorplan overlay for 'Apartment A' with hardcoded room rectangles and clickable hotspot dots; clicking a dot calls onPick with the dot id, and the active hub dot is highlighted.
observations: Rooms and hotspot dots are defined as two separate hardcoded arrays with percent-based coordinates.
The overlay background closes the modal on click, while clicks inside the card are stopped.
Dot buttons are absolutely positioned with transform: translate(-50%, -50%) and a border using a CSS custom property.
The component is assigned to window.FloorplanOverlay at the end of the file.
Inline styles are mixed with prefixed CSS classes (vh-).
No prop-types, TypeScript, or accessibility attributes are present.
standouts: Hardcoded floorplan data for a specific apartment.
Global window assignment of the React component.
conventions: Functional React component with destructured props.
CSS class naming prefix vh- for the virtual-hub UI kit.
Use of CSS custom properties for theming (e.g., --bg-canvas, --fg-secondary, --font-body).
Percent-based absolute positioning for responsive canvas layout.
concerns: Floorplan data is hardcoded, limiting reuse for other apartments.
Dot buttons have no accessible labels or ARIA attributes.
Inline style objects are recreated on every render.
Assigning the component to window may cause issues in non-browser or SSR environments.
No validation that onPick is a function beyond optional chaining.
notes: A lightweight, hardcoded apartment floorplan overlay with selectable hotspot dots for hub navigation.
questions: Is the floorplan data intended to be passed as props or loaded dynamically in a future version?
What does activeHub represent exactly — the currently viewed hub id or a selected state?
Are the CSS classes (vh-floorplan, vh-fp-room, vh-fp-dot) defined in a separate stylesheet?
Is this component expected to run in an SSR context given the window global assignment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of providing a floorplan overlay with selectable positions matches what the file does.
intention_signals: It appears designed to give users a visual map of an apartment and let them jump to specific hub viewpoints by tapping labeled hotspots.
novelty: It builds a simple, SVG-free floorplan using only divs and percent positioning, with separate room labels and interactive dots.
contribution: Provides a compact overlay component that combines a labeled floorplan canvas with clickable hotspot selection, useful as a spatial navigation helper in a virtual hub interface.
summary_for_embedding: FloorplanOverlay.jsx is a React functional component that renders a modal floorplan overlay with hardcoded apartment room rectangles and clickable hotspot dots, highlighting an active hub and invoking onPick when a dot is selected.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let users pick a location within an apartment floorplan to jump to the corresponding hub view.

symbols.does: Renders a clickable floorplan overlay with room labels and selectable hotspot dots.
symbols.description: FloorplanOverlay is a React functional component that displays a modal card containing a percent-positioned floorplan of Apartment A. It renders room rectangles from a hardcoded rooms array and interactive dot buttons from a dots array, applying an active class to the dot matching the activeHub prop and calling onPick with the dot id when clicked. The overlay closes when the backdrop is clicked, and the component is exposed globally as window.FloorplanOverlay.
